headline: Freeport McMoRan Stock Slides as the Iran Conflict Hits Commodities
summary: Freeport McMoRan  stock plummeted Tuesday, along with shares of other metals and mining companies, as commodities declined in a risk-off trade tied to U.S. military action in Iran.  Shares of the copper miner slid 10.1% in midday trading at $61.36, while the broader market struggled.

headline: 3 Reasons Gold Prices Are Falling Despite an Escalating Iran Conflict
summary: The price of gold pitched lower early Tuesday, even as the war in the Middle East ratcheted up.  Continuous gold futures  were down 2.5% to $5,181.1 an ounce, erasing Monday’s 1.2% gains and then some. The S&P 500 was trending sideways early Tuesday.  Gold’s decline would appear counterintuitive since it is typically thought of as a haven to geopolitical risks.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Newmont (NEM) After Its 209% One Year Rally?
summary: If you are wondering whether Newmont is actually good value at its current share price, this article will walk through what the numbers are really saying about the stock. The shares last closed at US$128.73, with returns of 3.6% over 7 days, 14.6% over 30 days, 27.2% year to date, 209.7% over 1 year, 216.3% over 3 years and 165.3% over 5 years. This performance has put valuation firmly in focus for many investors. Recent coverage around Newmont has centered on how this performance fits into...
